_comment: CANONICAL NAVIG tier / entitlement source of truth for the whole monorepo. The `live` section is the model we SELL + ENFORCE today (NAVIG Harbor: Free/Plus/Max + Team/Enterprise). Python (navig-core/navig/license/quota.py) and TypeScript (navig-api/src/lib/billing.ts, navig-deck/lib/license.ts) all mirror `live` and MUST agree — the parity test navig-core/tests/license/test_tiers_parity.py enforces it. The `legacy` section is the retired 7-tier model, kept only so old/stray tokens still verify; `live.legacy_to_harbor` maps a legacy tier up to its Harbor equivalent. See docs/BUSINESS-PLAN.md + docs/MONETIZATION.md.
_modules_note: Modules are INTERNAL capability flags only (the @requires_capability gate + deck APP_MODULE map). They are NO LONGER sold a-la-carte — capabilities are folded into Harbor tiers. module_onetime_usd is retained only to honor pre-existing perpetual module grants.

live._comment: The live NAVIG Harbor model. Free/Plus/Max are self-serve; Team/Enterprise are contact-us (self-serve Team = Phase 4). Plus and Max carry identical capabilities; they differ on AI allowance, host scale, priority relay, and the lifetime SKU (Max only).

prices_usd._comment: Hypotheses from MONETIZATION.md; Stripe SKUs created by setup-stripe.mjs. Free has no SKU; Team/Enterprise are contact-us.

legacy_to_harbor._comment: Map a retired legacy tier UP to its Harbor equivalent (>= value; nobody loses entitlements). Applied at verify time in keys.py for any old token.

legacy._comment: Retired 7-tier model. Kept ONLY so old/stray signed tokens still verify (capabilities <= their Harbor mapping). Never sold.
